name: ClassRepository

tasks.title: Write ClassRepository contract spec
tasks.body:
Create a contract spec for ClassRepository at .wolfcastle/docs/specs/. The contract should define:

- NewClassRepository(prompts *PromptRepository) constructor
- Reload(classes map[string]ClassDef) for goroutine-safe class list updates
- Resolve(key string) (string, error) with hierarchical fallback: 'lang-go' tries lang-go.md then lang.md; 'typescript/react' tries typescript/react.md then typescript.md
- List() []string returning sorted class keys
- Validate() []string returning class keys whose prompts are missing from all tiers

ClassRepository resolves class prompts through PromptRepository.ResolveRaw('prompts/classes', key+'.md'). It holds a sync.RWMutex for concurrent access: Reload takes write lock, Resolve/List/Validate take read lock.

Follow the contract spec format established by existing specs in .wolfcastle/docs/specs/.

tasks.title: Implement ClassRepository
tasks.body:
Implement ClassRepository in internal/pipeline/class_repository.go. The struct wraps PromptRepository and a goroutine-safe class map.

Constructor: NewClassRepository(prompts *PromptRepository) *ClassRepository

Methods:
- Reload(classes map[string]ClassDef): acquires write lock, replaces the internal class map. Called once at daemon startup with config.TaskClasses.
- Resolve(key string) (string, error): acquires read lock, looks up the class key, applies hierarchical fallback (split on '/' and try most-specific first, falling back to parent), then resolves the prompt file via PromptRepository.ResolveRaw('prompts/classes', matched+'.md'). Returns error if class key not found in map or prompt file missing.
- List() []string: acquires read lock, returns sorted keys from class map.
- Validate() []string: acquires read lock, iterates all configured classes, checks each has a resolvable prompt file, returns list of missing class keys.

ClassDef is defined in config; check existing type in internal/config/config.go. If it doesn't exist yet, define it there with whatever fields the task-classes spec requires.

tasks.title: Write ClassRepository tests
tasks.body:
Write tests for ClassRepository in internal/pipeline/class_repository_test.go using testutil.Environment.

Test cases:
- Resolve returns correct prompt content for a simple class key
- Resolve applies hierarchical fallback: 'lang-go' falls back to 'lang' when 'lang-go.md' is missing
- Resolve returns error for unknown class key (not in map)
- Resolve returns error when class key exists in map but prompt file missing from all tiers
- Reload replaces class map and subsequent Resolve reflects new classes
- List returns sorted keys
- Validate identifies classes with missing prompt files
- Validate returns empty slice when all classes have prompts

Use env.WithPrompt() to seed prompt files in the base tier. Create a ClassDef map to pass to Reload(). The testutil.Environment already has PromptRepository wired up.

tasks.title: Wire ClassRepository into testutil.Environment
tasks.body:
Add a Classes field (*pipeline.ClassRepository) to testutil.Environment. Initialize it in NewEnvironment() using the existing Prompts field:

  env.Classes = pipeline.NewClassRepository(env.Prompts)

Add a WithClasses(classes map[string]config.ClassDef) helper method that calls env.Classes.Reload(classes) and returns env for chaining.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in ClassRepository is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify ClassRepository.Resolve applies hierarchical fallback correctly: 'lang-go' falls back to 'lang', 'typescript/react' falls back to 'typescript'
Verify sync.RWMutex usage: Reload takes write lock, Resolve/List/Validate take read lock

breadcrumbs.text: Wrote ClassRepository contract spec covering constructor, Reload, Resolve (with hierarchical fallback), List, and Validate methods. Followed format established by ConfigRepository and PromptRepository contract specs.

Implemented ClassRepository in internal/pipeline/class_repository.go with Reload, Resolve (hierarchical fallback via / and - separators), List, and Validate methods. Added ClassDef type and TaskClasses field to config.Config. Wired ClassRepository into testutil.Environment.

Wrote 13 ClassRepository tests covering Resolve (simple, fallback-hyphen, fallback-slash, exact-precedence, unknown-key, missing-prompt, both-missing), Reload (map replacement), List (sorted, empty), and Validate (missing detection, all-present, fallback-as-present). All tests pass, full suite green.

Added WithClasses(map[string]config.ClassDef) helper to testutil.Environment. Calls env.Classes.Reload(classes) and returns env for chaining. Classes field and NewEnvironment initialization were already in place from the prior task.

Audit complete. Build, tests (including race detector), vet, and formatting all pass. Code review verified nil safety, error handling, concurrency, edge cases, naming, and modularity. One finding: missing ADR for hierarchical fallback strategy, created during audit. Spec is thorough and accurate. 13 tests cover all resolution paths, reload semantics, list ordering, and validation with fallback.

audit.result_summary: ClassRepository implements task class prompt resolution with a one-level hierarchical fallback chain (exact key, then parent via / or - separator). The implementation is goroutine-safe via sync.RWMutex, delegates all file I/O to PromptRepository.ResolveRaw, and provides Reload, Resolve, List, and Validate methods. ClassDef was added to config.Config, and WithClasses was added to testutil.Environment for test ergonomics. A 13-test suite covers all resolution paths (simple, hyphen fallback, slash fallback, exact-before-fallback, unknown key, missing prompts), reload semantics, sorted listing, and validation with fallback awareness. A contract spec documents the full API, error behavior, thread safety, and invariants. An ADR records the rationale for the one-level fallback design.
